entries.summary: Putin recognised Donetsk and Luhansk People's Republics as independent eastern Ukraine states.
Russia launched full invasion of Ukraine; siege of Mariupol began the same day.
Russia's defence ministry said Kherson city was under full Russian control.
Russian forces bombed Mariupol maternity hospital, killing four people including children.
Mariupol drama theatre attacked; Ukrainian officials reported more than 300 civilian deaths.
Ukraine won the battle for Kyiv as Russian forces withdrew from capital.
Putin ordered Russian troops to seal off Azovstal steelworks complex in Mariupol.
Mariupol fell as roughly 1,700 Azovstal defenders surrendered after 80-day stand.
Ukrainian forces ordered to withdraw from Severodonetsk in eastern Luhansk region.
Russian forces captured Lysychansk after intense bombardment, completing Luhansk takeover.
Turkey and UN brokered Black Sea grain export deal between warring parties.
Ukraine launched a major counteroffensive across the Kharkiv region.
Ukrainian forces recaptured strategic railway hub Izyum in eastern Ukraine.
Ukrainian forces recaptured Lyman in Donetsk region after Russian retreat.
Russia's defence minister announced military withdrawal from southern Ukrainian city Kherson.
Russia announced its forces had captured the salt-mining town of Soledar.
Russia took control of Bakhmut after one of war's bloodiest battles.
Wagner chief Prigozhin killed in plane crash northwest of Moscow, Russian officials said.
Russia said it had taken full control of Avdiivka in Donetsk region.
Zelensky signed expanded mobilisation law as Ukraine faced critical manpower shortages.
Ukrainian forces launched cross-border incursion into Russia's Kursk region.
Putin announced 'special military operation' as Russian forces launched full-scale invasion.
Zelensky declined US evacuation, telling Washington he needed ammunition, not a ride.
Russian forces bombed Mariupol drama theatre sheltering civilians, killing roughly 300.
Russian withdrawal from Bucha revealed civilian killings later called grave breaches.
Russian Black Sea flagship Moskva sank, the biggest Russian wartime naval loss.
Ukrainian forces recaptured Kupiansk during the rapid Kharkiv counteroffensive.
Ukraine retook Kherson, the only regional capital Russia had captured since invasion.
Russian missile strike on Dnipro apartment block killed more than 40 people.
Drones struck Kremlin in central Moscow; Ukraine denied involvement in attack.
Russia captured eastern Ukrainian city of Bakhmut after months-long siege.
Wagner chief Prigozhin led short-lived armed insurrection, marching forces towards Moscow.
Russian strike on Hroza café in Kharkiv region killed at least 51.
Zelensky dismissed top commander Zaluzhnyi in biggest military shakeup since invasion.
Russia said it had taken full control of Avdiivka in eastern Ukraine.
Ukrainian forces launched a cross-border incursion into Russia's Kursk region.
Zelensky said about 50,000 Russian troops, including North Koreans, gathered in Kursk.
